properties.clause_summary: Terms & Conditions form a binding contract between the customer and FirstUK Bank PLC

Money you remove from the account

Applicant must be a UK resident

Applicant must be able to provide proof of address

name: Account Opening Identity and Address Verification

properties.rule_description: Applicant must pass Anti-Money Laundering (AML) screening

properties.clause_summary: Account activation occurs after the application process is completed
properties.bank_obligations: Activate account within the specified timeframe following successful screening and verification

properties.feature_description: Ability to complete the application form through multiple entry points

properties.withdrawal_rights: either can withdraw full balance unilaterally

properties.rule_description: Both customers must complete separate identity verification
Both customers must pass AML screening separately

properties.clause_summary: Both account holders are responsible for the account
Either joint holder can withdraw the full balance

properties.verification_method: separate identity verification and separate AML screening

properties.feature_description: Ability to fund account via bank transfer
Ability to fund account using cash
Ability to fund account via check

properties.rule_description: A minimum deposit of £1.00 is required to open the account

properties.clause_summary: There is no upper limit on the total balance that can be held in the account
Deposits are credited to the account and available for use without a waiting period

properties.feature_description: Transfer of funds to another account

properties.conditions: Subject to cash availability at branch

properties.clause_summary: Customers have easy access to funds at any time with no restrictions
properties.customer_rights: Easy access to funds at any time

properties.dormancy_threshold: 12 months of no activity and no customer contact

properties.clause_summary: Conditions and processes for an account becoming dormant
bank_obligations: Send letter to last known address
Maintain full access for deposits and withdrawals
properties.customer_rights: Maintain full access to deposit or withdraw funds

properties.rule_description: Account must have no activity (deposits/withdrawals) and no customer contact for 12 months

properties.feature_description: Dormant accounts continue to earn interest and allow full deposit/withdrawal access
properties.conditions: Account must be marked as dormant

properties.clause_summary: Defines AER as the Annual Equivalent Rate, which demonstrates the yearly interest earned.
Interest is calculated on a daily basis and paid monthly on the last business day of each month.
properties.bank_obligations: Calculate interest daily and execute payment on the last business day of the month
properties.time_limits: Last business day of each month

properties.feature_description: Interest rates are applied based on the account balance as of the payment date.
properties.conditions: Tiered rates apply to the balance as of the payment date

properties.clause_summary: Policy governing the frequency and timing of interest rate adjustments

name: Variable Rate — Bank of England Base Rate + 2.5% margin

properties.rate_basis: Bank of England Base Rate + 2.5% margin

properties.feature_description: Automatic notification of interest rate changes

properties.clause_summary: Right for customers to move to an alternative product following a rate change
properties.customer_rights: Switch to different product if unhappy with rate

properties.trigger_event: Expedited delivery of replacement debit card

properties.feature_description: Set up a regular, fixed transfer to a payee

properties.clause_summary: Standing orders can be cancelled at any time with immediate effect

properties.reversal_rules: Refund within 10 business days if unauthorized

properties.clause_summary: Provides full protection and refunds for unauthorized direct debit withdrawals
customer_rights: Refund within 10 business days if unauthorized

properties.clause_summary: Process for terminating a direct debit authorization
properties.customer_obligations: Request from bank or contact biller directly

properties.withdrawal_rights: Either holder can withdraw full balance unilaterally
properties.closure_consent_required: Both holders must agree to account closure

properties.clause_summary: Both signatories are liable for the account
properties.customer_rights: Either account holder can withdraw the full balance

properties.clause_summary: Interest is shared equally between holders
properties.exception_or_caveat: Holders can specify a different split

properties.clause_summary: Account closure requires mutual agreement
properties.customer_obligations: Both holders must agree to close the account

properties.withdrawal_authority: Can act alone to withdraw full balance

properties.balance_rules: Estate of deceased entitled to 50% share upon death of one holder

properties.withdrawal_authority: Surviving holder retains full account access

properties.clause_summary: Provisions for account access and fund distribution following the death of a joint holder
properties.customer_rights: Surviving holder maintains full access to the account
properties.bank_obligations: Recognize the estate of the deceased as entitled to 50% of the account share

properties.rule_description: Bank requires a death certificate and supporting documentation to process the death of an account holder

properties.dispute_resolution: Civil matter; customers recommended to seek legal advice
properties.liability_type: Joint legal rights to full balance

properties.clause_summary: Both account holders retain legal rights to the full account balance upon separation
properties.customer_rights: Access to full account balance regardless of individual contribution

name: Joint Account Freeze and Closure Rule

properties.clause_summary: Either holder can freeze or close the account during separation
properties.customer_rights: Ability to unilaterally freeze or close account

properties.clause_summary: The bank does not determine individual ownership of funds in a joint account during separation

properties.withdrawal_authority: Either holder can freeze or close the account
properties.liability_scope: Both retain legal rights to full account balance

name: Personal Data Collection and Usage Policy

properties.clause_summary: Outlines the purposes for which the bank collects and uses personal customer data
customer_rights: Control over marketing usage via consent

properties.clause_summary: The customer has the right to request a copy of their personal data.
The customer has the right to correct inaccurate personal data.
The customer has the right to request the deletion of their personal data.
The customer has the right to receive their data in a portable format.
Overview of customer data protection rights under GDPR.

eligibility_criteria: Subject to GDPR data protection rights

properties.clause_summary: Outlines the conditions and entities with whom FirstUK Bank shares customer data.
bank_obligations: Share data with credit reference agencies for credit assessment
Share data with regulatory authorities (FCA, PRA) if required
Share data with law enforcement if court-ordered
properties.exception_or_caveat: The bank does not sell customer data to marketers

properties.clause_summary: Customers may close their account at any time using specific approved channels.
properties.customer_rights: Right to close account at any time
properties.customer_obligations: Must use one of the three specified closure methods

properties.clause_summary: The step-by-step sequence for closing an account, involving identity verification, balance calculation, mandate cancellation, and final disbursement.
bank_obligations: Calculate final balance including pending interest
Cancel Standing Orders and Direct Debits

properties.clause_summary: Documents related to closed accounts are archived for a set period.

name: Standing Order and Direct Debit Cancellation

properties.reversal_rules: Cancelled effective immediately upon account closure process

properties.closure_conditions: Can be closed by the bank for T&C breaches, suspected fraud/unauthorized use, regulatory orders, or AML requirement breaches

properties.clause_summary: The bank reserves the right to close the account under specific conditions
properties.bank_obligations: Provide 30 days notice except in emergencies
properties.customer_obligations: Adhere to terms & conditions and AML requirements
properties.exception_or_caveat: Notice period is waived in emergency situations

properties.clause_summary: The time frame the bank provides before closing an account

properties.rule_description: The customer must comply with Anti-Money Laundering requirements

properties.clause_summary: Process for customers to report dissatisfaction with service and the bank's timeline for responding.
bank_obligations: Respond within 8 calendar days for standard complaints
Respond within 8 weeks for complex complaints
Explain the decision and provide next steps
customer_obligations: Contact the bank via phone, email, or post

properties.question: What is the process if I am unhappy with the service?
properties.answer_summary: Customers should contact the bank via phone, email, or mail; the bank will respond within 8 days (or 8 weeks for complex issues) with a decision and next steps.
properties.customer_action: Contact the bank via 0800-555-2265 or support@firstuk.bank

properties.clause_summary: Process for escalating complaints to the Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS) if the customer is unhappy with the bank's resolution
customer_rights: Potential compensation up to £385,000 if the bank is at fault
bank_obligations: Provide access to independent dispute resolution via FOS

name: Complaint Escalation to Financial Ombudsman Service

properties.question: What happens if I am unhappy with the resolution of my complaint?
properties.answer_summary: Customers can escalate their complaint to the Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS), which is a free and independent service capable of awarding compensation up to £385,000.
properties.customer_action: Contact FOS via phone (0800 023 4567) or website (www.financialombudsman.org.uk)

properties.clause_summary: Definition of the bank's responsibilities and liability areas
The bank's liability for unauthorized transactions
bank_obligations: Responsibility for fraud and unauthorized transactions

properties.clause_summary: The bank's liability regarding breaches of customer confidentiality
The bank's liability for technical failures and system downtime
bank_obligations: Responsibility for system failures and outages

properties.clause_summary: The bank is not responsible for delays occurring within external payment schemes/systems.
properties.customer_rights: No recourse for delays caused by third-party payment schemes

name: PIN and Password Disclosure Liability Clause

properties.clause_summary: The bank is not liable for financial losses resulting from the customer disclosing their PIN or password.
properties.customer_obligations: Maintain confidentiality of PIN and password
properties.exception_or_caveat: Liability shifts to customer upon disclosure

properties.clause_summary: The bank is not responsible for fraud if the customer was negligent, such as leaving a card visible.
properties.customer_obligations: Exercise reasonable care in securing cards and account details
properties.exception_or_caveat: Bank liability is voided by customer negligence

name: Variable Interest Rate Change Liability Clause

properties.clause_summary: The bank is not liable for losses or dissatisfaction resulting from changes to interest rates on variable products.
The bank is not responsible for actions taken by third parties that the customer has authorized.
properties.customer_obligations: Responsible for the actions of parties they authorize to act on their behalf

properties.clause_summary: Outlines the security obligations of the customer to prevent fraud and unauthorized access.
customer_obligations: Report fraud or suspicious activity immediately
Promptly notify the bank of suspicious activity

properties.clause_summary: Process for refunding customers in the event of fraud following an investigation
properties.bank_obligations: Investigate reports and provide full refunds for unauthorized transactions

properties.clause_summary: Liability determination for transactions not authorized by the customer
properties.customer_rights: Full refund with no customer liability

properties.clause_summary: Determination of liability when customer negligence contributes to fraud
properties.exception_or_caveat: Liability may be shared between bank and customer on a case-by-case basis

properties.reversal_rules: Full refund if unauthorized; shared liability if customer negligent

properties.clause_summary: The bank reserves the right to modify the terms and conditions of the agreement under specific circumstances.
bank_obligations: Provide 30 days written notice before implementing changes
conditions: Change must be in the bank's or customer's legitimate interest
Change may be required to reflect regulatory updates

properties.clause_summary: Right to close account if the customer disagrees with changes to terms
properties.exception_or_caveat: Applies specifically if the customer disagrees with changes

properties.rule_description: Customer must close the account within 60 days if disagreeing with terms changes

properties.trigger_event: Closing the account due to disagreement with changes in terms
properties.conditions: Waived specifically for customers exercising their right to close due to term changes

properties.trigger_event: Transferring the final balance upon account closure
properties.conditions: Free if closed due to disagreement with changes in terms

properties.closure_conditions: Can be closed within 60 days if customer disagrees with terms changes

properties.fee_triggers: Typically chargeable, but free under Change of Terms Dispute Provision

properties.clause_summary: The Terms & Conditions constitute the complete agreement between the bank and the customer, superseding all previous agreements or representations.
properties.bank_obligations: The bank is only bound by the terms stated in this document rather than prior representations.
properties.customer_obligations: The customer acknowledges that prior agreements are superseded by these terms.

properties.clause_summary: If any specific term of the agreement is found to be invalid, the rest of the agreement remains legally binding and in effect.
properties.bank_obligations: The bank continues to operate under the remaining valid terms of the agreement.
properties.customer_obligations: The customer remains bound by all other valid terms of the agreement.
properties.exception_or_caveat: Applies only to the specific term found invalid.

properties.clause_summary: The agreement is governed by English Law and falls under the jurisdiction of the Courts of England & Wales.
properties.bank_obligations: Adherence to the legal framework of England & Wales
properties.customer_obligations: Submission to the jurisdiction of the Courts of England & Wales

properties.feature_description: Access to bank support via telephone, email, and physical branches

properties.clause_summary: Protection provided by the FSCS for account funds
Guarantee ensuring refunds for unauthorized Direct Debits
Standard procedure for handling customer complaints under FCA guidelines
properties.bank_obligations: Investigate complaints within a specific timeframe

properties.rule_description: Customer must be 18 years of age or older

properties.clause_summary: Customers must accept liability for joint accounts where applicable
properties.customer_obligations: Acceptance of liability for joint account holdings
